mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% year-over-year, or Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production index values reflect expanded manufacturing output and corporate capacity utilization, which translate into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns exhibit a negative or zero correlation with INDPRO levels across all tested rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1.5% while the unemployment rate remains stable, accommodative monetary policy offsets labor market slack, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in over 60% of triggered observations during periods of rising inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses the 2.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening employment conditions and higher wage growth, which increase consumer spending and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 270.0 while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, delayed monetary tightening expectations trigger risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 270.0 and FEDFUNDS stays below 1.0%.
